Architecting a Centralized Platform for Data Deletion at Netflix

November 17, 2025 · talks

How do you safely manage data deletion across a fleet of diverse data stores, at Netflix scale? In this QCon San Francisco 2025 talk, Shawn Liu and I present a centralized, extensible platform that owns the end-to-end data deletion lifecycle: from identifying data, to executing deletion across heterogeneous backends, to verifying it actually happened.

We discuss the architecture, the privacy and compliance constraints driving the design, and the resilience patterns that keep the platform reliable under failure.

Talk on QCon SF · InfoQ write-up

Photos by QCon Pictures.

← All talks

Staff Software Engineer at Netflix and founding architect of Netflix's Data Abstraction Platform. I write and speak about distributed databases, data abstractions, and platform engineering at scale.
